question_title: Will CTAS's market close price on 2026-04-23 be higher than its market close price on 2026-04-14?
summary: CTAS is a 7-trading-day stock direction question (April 14 close vs April 23 close). The key anchor is the conditional drawdown base rate: when CTAS has been down >=20% from its 52-week high (currently -23.4%), 7-day forward returns have been positive 58.8% of the time across 182 historical episodes. Monte Carlo using these conditional parameters gives 57.6%. The broader market has recovered strongly (S&P 500 up 8.5% from March lows, near 52w highs), supporting risk-on sentiment. CTAS itself has bounced 6% from its March 27 low with positive recent momentum. Offsetting factors include ongoing tariff uncertainty ($29B/month in tariff revenue impacting businesses) and the fact that some of the mean-reversion bounce has already occurred. Final estimate: 56%, modestly above the unconditional ~52% but slightly below the raw conditional base rate.

factors.description: Conditional base rate: when CTAS is down >=20% from 52w high, 7-day forward returns are positive 58.8% of the time (182 historical episodes since 1983)
Broader market recovery: S&P 500 has bounced 8.5% from March lows and is only 1.7% from 52w high, indicating strong risk-on sentiment
CTAS recent positive momentum: stock has recovered 6% from March 27 low, 5-day return +2.3%, consecutive positive sessions
Macro uncertainty from ongoing tariff/trade war: $29B/month in tariff revenue creating headwinds for consumer-facing sectors; CTAS serves businesses affected by trade disruptions
Zero-drift baseline: over 7 trading days with no informational edge, random walk gives 50% probability
Recent 20-day negative drift reflects crash period not representative of forward conditions; recovery already underway reduces its relevance

reasoning:
**Forecast: 56% probability that CTAS closes higher on April 23 than April 14.**

Key reasoning:
- **Conditional drawdown base rate** is the strongest signal: historically, when CTAS is down 20%+ from its 52-week high, 7-day forward returns are positive ~59% of the time (182 episodes)
- **Broad market recovery** supports upside — S&P 500 is near 52w highs after bouncing 8.5% from March lows
- **CTAS momentum** is positive (+6% from March 27 low, +2.3% over last 5 days)
- **Tariff uncertainty** provides a modest headwind
- **Some mean-reversion already consumed** — the 6% bounce reduces remaining upside potential, justifying a small discount from the raw 58.8% base rate
sources_consulted: [Cintas Corporation (CTAS) Stock Price, News, Quote & History - Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/CTAS/)
resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the latest market close price of CTAS. If it is higher than the close price on 2026-04-14, the question will resolve to 'Yes'. The close price will be pulled from the Yahoo Finance API.
fine_print: Stock splits and reverse splits will be accounted for in resolving this question. Forecasts on questions about companies that have been delisted (through mergers or bankruptcy) will resolve to their final close price.
